records.ety: [OF. rasper, F. rper, to scrape, grate, rasp, fr. OHG. raspōn to scrape together, to collect, probably akin to E. rap. Cf. Rap to snatch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rasped (rspt); p. pr. & vb. n. Rasping.]

defs: To rub or file with a rasp; to rub or grate with a rough file; as, to rasp wood to make it smooth; to rasp bones to powder.
Hence, figuratively: To grate harshly upon; to offend by coarse or rough treatment or language; as, some sounds rasp the ear; his insults rasped my temper.

records.ety: [OE. raspe, OF. raspe, F. rpe. See Rasp, v.]

defs: A coarse file, on which the cutting prominences are distinct points raised by the oblique stroke of a sharp punch, instead of lines raised by a chisel, as on the true file.
